home.subText: Scan your cube with a smartphone and get a step-by-step solution.

deviceSelect.permissionDeniedDesc: Allow camera access to use this app.
deviceSelect.instr3: Use the 3D preview to match your cube's orientation.

scan.anyOrientation: Any orientation works · Just fit it in the square
scan.cameraOpenErrorDesc: Check camera permission and make sure no other app is using it.
scan.scanFailToastDesc: One or more faces look misread. Please rescan the affected face.

manualInput.heading: Scramble your cube
manualInput.hint: Hover a sticker to highlight a layer, then drag in the direction you want it to rotate.

tutorial.title: How manual input works
tutorial.dragFaceBody: Rotates that layer. Hover first to see which layer will turn.
tutorial.dragEmptyBody: Orbits the camera. Use it to see the back faces.
tutorial.hoverBody: Move the cursor toward a cubie's top, bottom, left, or right edge to highlight a different layer. Both row and column are reachable from one cubie.
tutorial.resetBody: Restores the cube to the solved state.

solve.switchModeError: Couldn't generate a solution while switching modes.
solve.scanRescanDesc: This color layout can't be solved. Double-check the face colors and rescan.
solve.doubleHint: A 180° turn means two quarter-turns in the same direction. Try again.
solve.wrongFace: Wrong face. Try again, or press 💡 Hint.

0.goal: Place the 4 white edges on the bottom (D) face so each side sticker matches its adjacent center.
0.approach: Bring each white edge up to the U face, line it up above its target slot, and drop it down with F2 / R2 / B2 / L2.
0.tip: Each center color tells you that face's target color. Pair white with the neighboring center to choose the right slot.

1.goal: Line up the side colors of the bottom cross with their centers. (Merged with Step 1 in this solver.)
1.approach: This solver handles alignment during Step 1, so this step usually has 0 moves.

2.goal: Insert all 4 white corners into the bottom layer with the correct position and orientation.
2.approach: Bring the corner up to the U face, line it up above its slot, then repeat R U R' U' (right slot) or L' U' L U (left slot).
2.tip: The direction the white sticker is facing tells you how many trigger repeats you need (usually 1–5).

3.goal: Insert the 4 middle-layer edges. Edges containing yellow are saved for later.
3.approach: Find an edge on U with no yellow, line up its side color with the matching center, then run the right-hand or left-hand insert.
3.representativeAlgo:
Right: U R U' R' U' F' U F
Left:  U' L' U L U F U' F'

4.goal: Make the 4 U-face edge stickers yellow (positions U[1, 3, 5, 7]).
4.approach: Pattern flow: Dot → L → Line → Cross. Apply F R U R' U' F' once per pattern.
4.representativeAlgo: F R U R' U' F'
4.tip: Dot: any orientation works. L-shape: keep the L in the top-left. Line: hold it horizontally.

5.goal: Make every U-face sticker yellow (corners included).
5.approach: Repeat Sune (R U R' U R U2 R'). Apply 1–3 times depending on the case.
5.representativeAlgo: R U R' U R U2 R'  (Sune)
5.tip: 0 yellow corners: Sune from any orientation. 1 oriented corner: put it back-right and Sune. 2 corners: adjust by pattern first.

6.goal: Swap U-face corners so each one's side stickers match the surrounding centers.
6.approach: Keep one already-correct corner in place and cycle the other three with a PLL corner-swap algorithm.
6.tip: 0 correct corners: run the cycle once from any angle to create one. Then keep the correct corner at back-right.

7.goal: Swap U-face edges so each one's side stickers match the surrounding centers. Cube solved.
7.approach: U-perm (3-edge cycle). Pick the clockwise or counter-clockwise variant based on the cycle direction.
7.representativeAlgo:
CW:  R2 U R U R' U' R' U' R' U R'
CCW: R U' R U R U R U' R' U' R2
7.tip: Place the already-correct edge above the F face and apply. If none match, run it once from any angle to create one.

0.goal: Orient every edge so the rest of the solve never needs an F or B quarter-turn (enter group G1).
0.approach: A full HTM search orients all 12 edges in a single pass.
0.tip: From here on, F and B can only be used as half-turns (F2 / B2).

1.goal: Orient the corners (CO) and place the E-slice edges to reach the domino state (G2).
1.approach: Restricted search (U and D free, plus L, R, F2, B2) fixes corner orientation and E-slice edges.
1.tip: In the domino state the whole cube can be finished using only the top and bottom layers.

2.goal: Arrange the pieces into a group (G3) reachable using only half-turns.
2.approach: Restricted search (U and D free, plus F2, B2, L2, R2) places corners and edges into the even-permutation group.

3.goal: Finish the cube using only half-turns (G4 = solved).
3.approach: Use only U2, D2, L2, R2, F2, B2 to complete the permutation.
3.tip: Because this stage only uses 180° turns, the finish looks especially clean.

trackedSolve.title: Camera-tracked solve
trackedSolve.placeholder: Coming soon — after mapping the cube, the camera will follow your moves in real time and guide the solution.
trackedSolve.calibrating: Learning colors — slowly rotate the cube to show all 6 faces
trackedSolve.cvLoadFailed: Cube detector failed to load — check your network and retry
trackedSolve.showSolveFace: Couldn't read the move — point the camera straight at the face you're turning
